checks.finding: Every in-scope requirement (REQ-001..REQ-006) specifies concrete, observable outcomes: exact exit codes, literal terminal strings ('loop finished without reproducing (5 iterations)', 'RED: symptom reproduced on iteration 3'), file:line-cited state-machine rules (SKILL.md:44-50,119-135,186-203), SHA-256 before/after invariants (AC-005), and named-SKIP-to-green conversions (AC-013, AC-016) with recorded before/after counts. No in-scope requirement rests on unmeasurable intent.

Each of the three stated Problems and REQ-001..REQ-006 maps to explicit ACs: REQ-001 -> AC-001..AC-006; REQ-002 -> AC-007..AC-010 (plus AC-018); REQ-003 -> AC-011..AC-013; REQ-004 -> AC-014..AC-016; REQ-005 -> AC-017 (cross-referenced in AC-006/AC-010/AC-013/AC-016); REQ-006 -> AC-019. No goal is left untraced in acceptance-tests.md's AC-to-REQ table.

AC-001..AC-019 (specs/epic-159-pillar-a2/acceptance-tests.md:10-28) each name a specific test target, exit code, printed string, file existence, or byte-level hygiene check (e.g. AC-012/AC-015: zero non-ASCII bytes, no BOM, no CR) rather than vague intent. Round-3's AC-008 wording ('excluding BOTH marker-bearing files: the NotImplementedError base-class file and the TODO file') further sharpens observability versus round-2.

Non-goals (requirements.md:209-245) enumerate 7 explicit exclusions with rationale: no loop-inventory re-registration, no end-to-end skill/gh-issue-create exercise, no Audit-Attempt>=3 direction on real docs (with the reason no such real document exists), no re-litigating #127, no extending downstream-review-precheck-parity to spec/domain, no touching protected-gate files, and tasks.md/traceability.md/Pillar B explicitly deferred.

Security Boundaries table (requirements.md:493-498, B1-B4) states mktemp isolation, real-file-never-written guarantees, and no-network-reachability; Assumptions section pins inventory/dispatch/grep-exit-code stability; Edge Cases enumerate bash-3.2 array safety (INV-029), macOS TMPDIR normalization (INV-030), Windows jq CRLF handling (INV-031), and real-validator CRLF fragility gating (INV-032) as binding constraints on any new code.

Risks section (requirements.md:557-584) ties each risk to a concrete mitigation and AC: Critical risk (skill-logic drift) -> AC-005's real-document cross-check; High risk (broken CHECK wiring) -> AC-002's mandatory canary; Medium risks -> AC-010's positive non-SKIP assertion and design.md's documented commit-serialization precedent.
